item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me of the past was a little too grumpy. This is okay. Downvote cancelled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looking back at this, I can see how my SCPs were based on my interests at the time. I worked somewhere I had to bike to daily, so I liked the idea of someone stealing it and getting their comeuppance.

And I still love this little guy. Short, simple, but it works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
#bikelivesmatter «I joke

+1 I really enjoyed this SCP. It has a deeper story behind it, like a bike community or conspiracy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.

Well that depends on whether it is sentient or automated.

If it is automated, its reaction will be the same as if someone was stealing it.

If it is sentient, then it might just let you off with a bruise. From its perspective, any unauthorized use of the bike is stealing…but there is some ambiguity, so it probably won't kill you.

I'll leave the question of whether it is aware or not up in the air.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's say you absolutely need this bicycle to escape from something threatening your life. How will SCP-1365 react?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BIKE JUSTICE: +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome so far. It could use some fleshing out, such as examples of tests, and where it was found (black-inked of course)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After ten minutes or 500 metres of travel, whichever is sooner

500 meters will likely always be sooner. For 500 meters to take more than 10 minutes you have to travel at less than 3 km/h, which is a) kinda difficult and b) not a harmful speed to fall at.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: XD this is great! The words "BIKE JUSTICE" remind me of my very favorite youtuber, AlChestBreach, and his propensity for such goofy ideas. I like this. It's a good example of how an anomalous object can be funny and still be a mainlister. I give this my blessing, with a +1 Upvote of the Writer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted due to the long history I have with my bicycles being stolen from me. Kept the upvote because of the stable and coherent writing within.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A good trap for bike thieves :-)
Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First thing that caught my eye. "Abilities", etc. anything would be fine. Not "powers".

Actually, I would say "properties" since we're talking about an inanimate object here, not a sentient or intelligent entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea isn't that interesting (SCP object that hurts you and reappears), but BIKE JUSTICE saves it from being mediocre. That and having no compulsion, just being a thief magnet. Simple, effective and gets you thinking.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral on this.
I like the idea of BIKE JUSTICE but I don't think it's too well executed here.
I'm also kinda getting sick of skips vanishing to the nearest [place with arbitrary condition].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
neutralizing SCP-1365's teleportation powers

First thing that caught my eye. "Abilities", etc. anything would be fine. Not "powers".

it is nonetheless frequently activated if left uncontained, likely due to its pristine condition and the lack of anti-theft measures normally taken to protect a bike.

This sounds quite silly, especially with the "likely" in there. "So, Doctor, do you have any new, exciting theory as to why do people like to steal bikes?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too straightforward, really just another thing what kills you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Q: When is a bike rack not a bike rack?
A: When it's a jar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This leaves me cold, so no vote either way.

Question: If it is built exactly like a bike rack, and there is a bike racked it in, is it still a mockup of a bike rack?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is besauce I dyxlesic. (Although 2006-J also uses undiscriminating righteous smiting in order to make justice triumph over evildoers, so it's the more similar of the two.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's SCP-2600-J. SCP-2006-J is a different article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty straightforward and boring, and the writing doesn't do much for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bike justice! I like this far more than the similar -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed "terminate" to "killed" since it bothered so many people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Used "terminate" incorrectly and is a Vehicle What Kills You with a side dish of Disproportionate Retribution?

Colour me -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't have to do with the D-class issue at all. "Terminate" is used as a bureaucratic euphemism any time the Foundation deliberately executes someone. Regardless of your position on whether the Foundation actually terminates D-classes at the end of the month, it still refers to a deliberate action the Foundation is taking, not the actions of an SCP object ending with someone dying. Therefore, it is always incorrect to refer to an SCP object's effects happening to kill someone as it "terminating" them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: BIKE JUSTICE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I also thought of 2600-J when I saw the title, but you could say it's really the opposite of the Bike Mafia. It's well written and I like short and sweet SCPs like that, but it did not make me feel any specific emotion, apart from a slight amusement. So no vote for now. Personally I see it more as entrapment rather than real justice, so I feel less positively toward this bicycle.

Would ''bicycle'' be a more accurate word rather than ''bike'' in the text. ''Bike'' feels less professional and also when I see ''bike'' I usually think of a motorcycle.

likely due to its pristine condition and the lack of anti-theft measures normally taken to protect a bike.

Maybe you should state that at the beginning of the description, because you only say that it is unremarquable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, termination is simply the action of bringing something to an end, such as the termination of a contract or termination of contract employment, which is why it is a good euphemism for killings. Morale would plummet in a supposedly scientific institution if such deaths weren't abstracted in some way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amusing concept. I enjoyed reading
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but it feels like it should be in Family Guy, not the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it is more a joke about a bike rolling away with its hands crossed behind its back(metaphorically) whistling to appear innocent than an actual suggestion…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not against SCPs that are incredibly straight forward in their intent like this. My "but…" point is that this is not just an anomalous object with an agenda. From the looks of it, it's more like an anomalous object build by someone with an agenda. And this entity is much absent from this article. (Probably because, for all the author intended, there's no such entity; but I wish there was, because it just fits).

Also, how did the Foundation come across this? The addendum notes an activation event and a test subject, but doesn't really let us in on why the Foundation even started testing this in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit too informal, especially the "exhudes an aura" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
then I'd probably find myself either seeing the sticker as the SCP (which it might be, of course)

The article sounds clear to me that this is not the case, but I'd like to see that it is. Not only because it'd shift the SCP from "anomalous bike with a vengeance" to "sticker that imbues the sense of justice into a bike", but also it'd add another component to it: the ability to spread or transfer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right, but I've read several tales where "termination" has been used to mean "end of employment" without meaning death. That's also what I'm talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Three to four hours after the termination or incapacitation of the subject, SCP-1365 will spin its wheels and roll away from its current location towards the nearest location designed for the temporary storage of bikes in public places. Witnesses report to hear whistling that does not seem to match any melody in particular, and exhudes an aura of innocence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the D-Class discussion is really what Scantron was talking about. "Terminate" has been a pretty standard bureaucratic euphemism for killings carried out the Foundation's name, a distancing obfuscation.

item.title: Well, that's what termination means here in the context of the site.
item.content:encoded:
Going further… "Monthly recycling" doesn't exactly make much sense anyway; it's not like wiping someone's memory removes physical traces of contact with something, and cycling them to something else brings up the cross-contamination issue; in this case, there's literally no point to wiping memory anyway.

If you want an intelligent point of view, one might consider that there's really no point in executing them monthly anyway, and remember that the idiocy of past writers isn't something you have to hold yourself to; you can do better than them. Be smarter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume you mean "doesn't mean you don't have valid input".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, just cause you aren't an author doesn't mean you have valid input. Hell, I am an author and my comments are better off ignored 90% of the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is way too straight forward for me, and the core idea isn't interesting enough to be worth playing like that. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally see this as completely different from 2600-J (the only similarity being that they involve bicycles and people getting hurt with one being a cursed bicycle and one being sentient bicycles), but to each their own.

The teleportation is a bit iffy, but I can see it being part of the enchantment / whatever that was used to make this bike what it is. Personally, I think the article could stand on its own without teleportation or "bike magically reconstructs itself", although then I'd probably find myself either seeing the sticker as the SCP (which it might be, of course) or this being something still actively manufactured in the world and therefore not fully under control of the Foundation. :-P But enough about me, author of no articles, saying how you should do this. I upvoted it. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is definitely one of those words who's meaning has become obscured.

Plus, some of us (myself included) just don't include the monthly D-class termination at all in our headcanon; it really doesn't make much sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple, yet effective idea. I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BIKE JUSTICE!

I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Terminate" is specifically a euphemism for when the Foundation kills a person.

This is becoming a more controversial point recently, don't you think? "Monthly Termination" for a lot of users seems to mean a monthly rotation of remaining D assets, pumping them full of amnestics and starting their "one month" over again without them realizing they had ever lost it.

Also, for employees, termination could just mean they were either fired or retired. There's no reason to kill everyone who ever worked for the Foundation when they could dodge so many more ethical bullets by erasing their memories and/or keeping an eye on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Terminate" is specifically a euphemism for when the Foundation kills a person. In this situation, you can use "kill" or the like.

As for the article itself, if the gist is that someone's really mad at people who steal bikes, then it's not feeling particularly original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not accusing you of plagiarism. There are enough differences that this is clearly an independent work. However, there are not enough differences for it to add sufficient value to the site.

Tough break, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly had not read that before I posted this. A Newton-Leibniz incident, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you mean SCP-2600-J. And it reminds you of that because it's about 3/4ths of the same thing.

-1. Not sufficiently original.

e: Okay, not quite 3/4ths similar. But REALLY similar in the whole "no control over riding it, causing serious injury" thing. I stand by my vote.

e2: Upon reflection… I was being a fuddy-duddy that day. This is clever enough to live. Downvote retracted. My stance is now neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do envy the fact of how this idea comes straight forward, but I agree with DrSevere, your tone is a little shaky. But overall, I enjoy this anomaly as it is. So take this upvote:

+1

EDIT: And congratulations, your SCiP made me have Queen's song "Bicycle Race" stuck in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of SCP-2006-J somehow. Amusing, but not enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't been this torn on an SCP in a while

On one hand, I really like this. It's simple, straightforward, and kinda funny. No attempt was made to make it overly scary, which I like. Also, I'm a cyclist so I love the karmic justice.

That said, the tone is a bit off, I think the note at the end could use some work, and I feel like there is potential to develop the personality of the bike (or it's creator) that you didn't fully capitilize on. As well, the teleportation bit is kind of weak; I see a lot of SCPs include teleportation as their means of being dangerous, and I just feel like there is a better way to execute that which better keeps in the spirit of the SCP. I mean, it's a bicycle. It can roll. Teleporation seems almost sacreligious.

I want to upvote this, but with the issues I just can't. I will, however, be eagerly checking back every few hours to see if improvements have been made.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was hoping it turned them into bike knights like in Jackass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My attempt to fill the "cursed" slot of 1365. Less than an hour from conception to feedback to posting; the idea came to me, and I had to run with it.
